name: O-tiling
description:
O-Tiling is a free, open-source auto-tiling extension for GNOME Shell 48, 49, and 50. It automatically organizes your open windows into a clean, tiled layout — no manual dragging needed. Built on a proven tiling engine with many new features on top.

🔵 Active Focus Border
A smooth animated border highlights your focused window. Picks up your GNOME system accent color automatically (Blue, Teal, Green, Red, Purple, etc.) or accepts a custom color. Adjustable border width (1–10 px), corner radius (0–30 px), outer glow, window tint overlay with opacity control, custom overlay color, and flexible tint targets (focused window only or all tiled windows). Flicker-free: guard clauses prevent redundant border updates during rapid window switching.

🖼️ Workspace Overview Styling
Replaces the default workspace thumbnail bar with a fully custom version: auto-scaling thumbnails based on screen width and workspace count, auto-scroll to the active workspace, transparent background, accent-color border on the active workspace card, and configurable rounded corners.

🔢 Interactive Panel Workspace Switcher
Optional pill-shaped numbered buttons in the top panel — one per workspace. Click to switch instantly. The active workspace button uses your GNOME accent color. Includes an overview-toggle button with a custom symbolic icon.

🚫 Skip Overview on Startup
Go straight to your desktop after login. No Activities screen in the way.

🪟 Transparent Panel
Make the GNOME top panel transparent. Set any opacity from 0% to 100%, with an optional blur-style dark gradient backdrop so text stays readable on bright wallpapers.

🎨 Theme Consistency
Applies uniform corner styles (rounded or sharp) to GTK 3, GTK 4, and GNOME Shell components simultaneously — no User Themes extension required.

🔀 Layout Presets
One-click window rearrangement: Columns, Stacked (rows), Grid (2×2 / 2×3 / 3×2), and Spiral (alternating splits).

📌 Lock Master Window
Pin the left (main) window so it never gets split. New windows always tile into the right side. Master window holds at least 35% of the screen width.

🗂️ Per-Workspace Tiling Toggle
Enable or disable tiling for just the current workspace without affecting others.

⚙️ Core Tiling Features
Binary-tree layout engine per monitor and per workspace. Stacking / tabbed mode. Smart gaps (disappear when only one window is open). Full multi-monitor and hot-plug support. Keyboard-first: move, resize, swap, and rotate tiles without a mouse.

See the full README and changelog at: https://github.com/oliwebd/o-tiling
author: oliwebd
